results.title: Off the news: More tax revenue, but more expenses
results.description: The state’s Council on Revenues, which forecasts tax collections feeding Hawaii’s general fund, projects slightly more money will come in through June 30, 2027. The revenue total is expected to be $9.8 billion rather than $9.7 billion, giving Gov. Josh Green extra moolah to work with in scrutinizing line items on next year’s budget. Note, however, that Green cut $110 million from last year’s budget. So ... did we break even?

results.title: Off the news: Park service to stream USS Arizona dive
results.description: Criticism has swirled over FBI Director Kash Patel’s “VIP snorkel” activity near the sunken USS Arizona battleship at Pearl Harbor last summer. The WWII watery grave is hallowed ground — so while VIP trips do rarely occur, the optics weren’t good since Patel had not initially disclosed the excursion nor reason for it.

results.title: Editorial cartoon for Monday, May 25, 2026

results.description: The class of 2026 was selected for the Hawaii Hall of Honor on Sunday. The inductees were selected by a panel of media and administrators.

results.title: Hawaii jobless rate slips to third lowest in nation
results.description: Hawaii’s unemployment rate, which earlier this year was tied for the lowest in the country, has lost ground for three straight months and now sits nationally at 2.5%.

results.title: Letter: State idles as nature reclaims Hawaii highways
results.description: Many states routinely resurface commuter highways to protect vehicles and extend the life of the road. The Hawaii Department of Transportation (HDOT) appears to have pioneered a different strategy: Let the highway completely fall apart first and see what survives.

results.title: Letter: Trump sway over DOJ too broad for comfort
results.description: Todd Blanche was Donald Trump’s defense lawyer in New York, representing him in multiple criminal cases including his hush money trial. Then he was picked by Trump to be the acting attorney general of the Justice Department.

results.description: On June 15 at 2 p.m., at the courthouse in Kailua-Kona, a motion-to- dismiss hearing will be held involving Kamehameha Schools’ proposed Keauhou Bay bungalow resort development and the challenged final environmental impact statement.

results.title: Letter: Mayor should select HPD chief after 3 failures
results.description: Regarding the new HPD chief, in recent memory the Honolulu Police Commission has selected Louis Kealoha, Susan Ballard and Arthur “Joe” Logan, while — sometimes repeatedly — rejecting Mike Lambert, Ben Moszkowicz and Rade Vanic. The selectees share the common attribute of ultimately failing in the job, while the rejects share the not-so-common attribute of succeeding when they served as head of a local police or law enforcement department.

results.title: Letter: Flavored vape order has dire isle consequences
results.description: The Trump administration has ordered the FDA to allow marketing of flavored vapes (“Flavored favor,” Star-Advertiser, May 17) . This decision endangers our keiki. Big Tobacco says that transferring tobacco addiction to nicotine addiction through vaping reduces harm. This weak argument collapses in the case of fruit-flavored vapes. They are, basically, a gateway to nicotine addiction for adolescents, generating a steady source clientele for Big Tobacco. Nine out of 10 youth who vape use flavored products. Fruit-flavored vaping is linked to higher rates of continuous, long-term use among teens.

results.title: Shark sighting at Hanalei Bay, officials advise against ocean activities

results.title: ‘Potential crack’ in California chemical tank may prevent explosion, fire official says
results.description: An industrial tank in Southern California containing about 7,000 gallons of a highly flammable toxic chemical appears to have cracked, officials reported Sunday. The development was interpreted as a possible sign that a catastrophic explosion or rupture might yet be averted as tens of thousands of evacuees waited to return home. The post ‘Potential crack’ in California chemical tank may prevent explosion, fire official says appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: China sends astronaut on year-long space mission as it eyes 2030 moon landing
results.description: JIUQUAN, China — China sent three astronauts to its space station on Sunday, one of whom will stay for a year, a record length for the country, enabling the study of long-duration human physiology in space as Beijing works towards its ambition of a crewed moon landing by 2030. results.title: The American divide exposes the high GDP fallacy
results.description: The American economy is a wonder. The Economist observed that average wages in America’s poorest state, Mississippi, are higher than those in Britain, Canada and Germany. American GDP per capita now runs roughly 40% above western Europe. Post-pandemic productivity growth has been significantly faster than that of the eurozone. The consensus is settled: the American economy is the model. The post The American divide exposes the high GDP fallacy appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .
